> **Leitsatz:** Tabellen und Kerninteraktion nah an Production. Hardware → Text-Input. Integrationen → CSV-Upload. Mobile → Web. Multi-User → Demo-User. Multi-Institution → eine Institution.

| Production-Realität | Prototype-Ersatz | |
|---|---|---|
| Kamera-Scanner (iOS/Android) | Text-Input: Token manuell eingeben oder aus Liste wählen | Simuliert |
| NFC-Tap / Wallet-Scan | Gleicher Text-Input | Simuliert |
| CRM-Sync (Blackbaud, Tessitura …) | CSV-Upload für Member-Import; kein Live-Sync | Simuliert |
| Multi-Station, Multi-Staff | Ein Demo-User, eine Station-ID hardcoded | Weggelassen |
| Post-Visit SMS (2–6h Delay) | Button „SMS senden" loggt Intent, zeigt Preview | Simuliert |
| Network Reporting (NARM/ASTC) | Nicht enthalten | Weggelassen |
| Pricing / Feature Flags | Nicht enthalten | Weggelassen |
| Offline-Mode | Nicht enthalten | Weggelassen |
| Datenmodell (Guests, Visits, Members) | **Production-nah** — echte Tabellen, echte Relationen | Production |
| Credential Resolution Engine | **Production-nah** — Token → Typ → Entitlement → Outcome | Production |
| Entry Dashboard | **Production-nah** — Live-Daten aus Visit-Tabelle | Production |

---

## 2 — Datenmodell (Production-Fundament)

Diese Tabellen sind das Fundament. Sie werden im Prototyp exakt so gebaut, wie sie später in Production stehen — Felder, Typen, Relationen. Was hier steht, wird nicht weggeworfen.

### members

Import via CSV. Repräsentiert den CRM-Stand der Institution.

```
members
├── id                    uuid        PK
├── institution_id        uuid        FK → institutions (hardcoded im Prototyp)
├── external_crm_id       string      ID aus Blackbaud/Tessitura/etc.
├── name                  string
├── email                 string
├── phone                 string      nullable
├── membership_tier       enum        basic | family | patron | vip
├── membership_status     enum        active | expired | lapsed
├── membership_expires_at date
├── guest_pass_total      int         pro Membership-Periode
├── guest_pass_used       int
├── reciprocal_networks   string[]    ["NARM", "ASTC"]

└── imported_at           timestamp
```

### guests

Netto-neue Entität. Existiert heute nirgendwo in Cuseum.

```
guests
├── id                    uuid        PK
├── guest_token           string      unique — Barcode auf Guest Pass
├── referrer_member_id    uuid        FK → members
├── phone                 string      captured bei Wallet-Add
├── wallet_added_at       timestamp
├── institution_id        uuid
├── status                enum        active | consumed | expired
└── created_at            timestamp
```

### visits

Kern-Audit-Trail. Jeder Scan-Event wird hier geschrieben.

```
visits
├── id                    uuid        PK
├── token                 string      gescannter Barcode
├── credential_type       enum        dm_pass | guest_pass | day_ticket
├── member_id             uuid        nullable — FK → members
├── guest_id              uuid        nullable — FK → guests
├── outcome               enum        admit | deny | override
├── override_reason       string      nullable — Pflicht bei override
├── fraud_flags           string[]    ["duplicate_scan", "expired_override"]
├── station_id            string      hardcoded im Prototyp
├── staff_id              string      hardcoded im Prototyp

```

### fraud_rules (Konfigurationstabelle)

```
fraud_rules
├── id                    uuid
├── rule_type             enum        duplicate_scan | expired_override | guest_reuse | token_mismatch
├── window_minutes        int         z.B. 30 min für Duplicate Detection
├── severity              enum        info | warning | critical
└── active                boolean
```

---

## 3 — Die Kerninteraktion: Scan → Resolve → Decide → Log

Das ist der eine Moment, der den Wert beweist. Alles andere im Prototyp existiert, um diesen Flow zu ermöglichen oder sein Ergebnis sichtbar zu machen.

**01 — Token eingeben.** Der User tippt einen Barcode-String in ein Textfeld (simuliert den Kamera-Scan). Alternative: Dropdown mit vorhandenen Tokens aus der Demo-Datenbank.

**02 — Credential Resolution.** Das System identifiziert den Token-Typ (DM-Pass, Guest Pass, Day Ticket), schlägt die zugehörige Member- oder Guest-Entität nach und prüft Entitlement: Mitgliedschaft aktiv? Guest Pass noch nicht consumed? Duplicate Scan innerhalb des Zeitfensters?

**03 — Outcome-Anzeige mit Kontext.** Drei Zustände — grün / gelb / rot — jeweils mit spezifischem Kontext und empfohlener Aktion. Kein nacktes „Pass/Fail" mehr.

**04 — Admit / Override / Deny.** Ein Klick schreibt den Visit-Record. Bei Override: Pflicht-Dropdown für Reason. Bei Guest Pass: Status wechselt zu „consumed".

**05 — Fraud Detection.** Parallel zur Resolution prüft die Engine: Wurde dieser Token in den letzten X Minuten bereits gescannt? Wird ein expired Pass mit Override durchgewunken? Wird ein Guest Pass ein zweites Mal verwendet? Flags werden in `visits.fraud_flags` geschrieben.

**06 — Dashboard-Update.** Der neue Visit-Record erscheint sofort im Entry Dashboard — Live-Counter, Credential-Breakdown, Flags-Queue.

### Outcome-Zustände

**✓ ADMIT (grün)** — Membership aktiv, Guest Pass gültig, oder Day Ticket valid. Kontext: Name, Tier, verbleibende Guest Passes.

**⚠ REVIEW (gelb)** — Membership expired < 30 Tage, Guest Pass vom letzten Zyklus, Reciprocal-Claim ohne Match. Kontext: Ablaufdatum, Empfehlung, Renewal-Option.

**✕ DENY (rot)** — Membership lapsed > 30 Tage, Token unbekannt, Duplicate Scan, Guest Pass consumed. Kontext: Grund, letzte Aktivität, Override-Option.

---

## 4 — Screens

Der Prototyp hat drei Screens. Kein Login, kein Settings, kein Onboarding.

### Screen A — Scan Station (Hauptinteraktion)

Fullscreen-Fokus auf das Textfeld. Nach Token-Eingabe: sofortiger Wechsel zur Outcome-Anzeige mit Member/Guest-Kontext. Admit-Button prominent. Override-Flow als Secondary Action. Nach Admit: kurze Bestätigung, dann Reset für nächsten Scan.

Elemente: Token-Textfeld mit Autofocus, Outcome-Card (grün/gelb/rot) mit Kontext-Feldern (Name, Tier, Membership-Status, verbleibende Guest Passes, Ablaufdatum), Admit-Button, Override-Dropdown, letzte 5 Scans als Mini-Log unten.

### Screen B — Entry Dashboard

Perspektive des Membership-Koordinators. Sieht, was vorher unsichtbar war.

Elemente: Live Entry Count (heute), Guest Pass Count, Flags Count, Credential-Type-Breakdown (DM / Guest / Day Ticket), stündliche Traffic-Kurve für den aktuellen Tag, Flags-Queue (Duplicates, Overrides, Expired Admissions) mit Detail-Expand, Recent Entry Log mit Filter nach Credential Type und Outcome.

### Screen C — Data Setup

CSV-Upload für Member-Daten. Mapping-UI: User ordnet CSV-Spalten den Member-Feldern zu. Nach Import: Übersicht mit Anzahl importierter Records, Validierungsfehler. Zusätzlich: einfaches Formular zum manuellen Anlegen von Guest Passes (Token generieren, Referrer-Member auswählen).

---

## 5 — Credential Resolution Engine

Die Engine ist das Herz des Prototyps. Sie muss production-nah sein, weil die gesamte Scan-Logik auf ihr aufbaut.

```
// resolve(token)

1. Token Lookup
   → Suche in members.dm_token
   → Suche in guests.guest_token
   → Kein Match → return { outcome: DENY, reason: "unknown_token" }

2. Duplicate Check
   → SELECT FROM visits WHERE token = ? AND scanned_at > (now - window)
   → Match → flag "duplicate_scan", outcome abhängig von Severity-Config

3. Entitlement Check (je nach credential_type)
   DM Pass:
     → membership_status == active? → ADMIT
     → expired < 30d? → REVIEW + renewal_hint
     → lapsed > 30d? → DENY
   Guest Pass:
     → guest.status == active? → ADMIT + set consumed on admit
     → guest.status == consumed? → DENY + "already_used"
     → guest.status == expired? → DENY
   Day Ticket:
     → valid_date == today? → ADMIT
     → else → DENY

4. Context Assembly
   → Member: name, tier, expires_at, guest_passes_remaining
   → Guest: referrer_name, referrer_tier, wallet_added_at
   → Return: { outcome, credential_type, context, fraud_flags }
```

---

## 6 — Demo-Datensatz

Der Prototyp kommt mit vorinstallierten Demo-Daten, die alle Outcome-Pfade abdecken.

| Persona | Setup | Erwarteter Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| Maria Chen — Patron | Active, Tier: Patron, 4/6 Guest Passes übrig | 🟢 Admit, zeigt Tier + Guest Pass Count |
| Tom Rivera — Basic | Expired vor 12 Tagen | 🟡 Review, zeigt Ablaufdatum + Renewal-Hint |
| Sarah Kim — Family | Lapsed vor 4 Monaten | 🔴 Deny |
| Guest von Maria | Active Guest Pass, Phone captured | 🟢 Admit, markiert als consumed |
| Guest von Maria (2. Scan) | Gleicher Token, bereits consumed | 🔴 Deny + Flag "guest_reuse" |
| Tom Rivera (2. Scan in 5 min) | Override beim 1. Mal, jetzt Duplicate | 🔴 Deny + Flag "duplicate_scan" |
| Unknown Token | Token existiert nicht | 🔴 Deny, "unknown_token" |

---

## 7 — Explizit nicht enthalten

Diese Entscheidungen sind bewusst. Jedes dieser Features wird erst gebaut, wenn der Kernflow validiert ist.

| Feature | Warum nicht |
|---|---|
| Native Mobile App | Hardware-Scan ist simuliert. Mobile kommt erst, wenn die Datenlogik steht. |
| CRM Live-Sync | Jede CRM-Integration ist institution-spezifisch. CSV-Import reicht zum Beweisen. |
| Post-Visit SMS | SMS-Versand ist ein Twilio-Call. Im Prototyp: Button zeigt Preview + loggt Intent. |
| GPR+ Attribution Funnel | Braucht echte Guest-Conversions über Wochen. Wird als statischer Report mit Demo-Daten angedeutet. |
| Network Reporting | Pro-Tier Feature. Nicht relevant für Kern-Validierung. |
| Multi-Institution | Eine Institution reicht. Mandantenfähigkeit ist Architektur, nicht Prototyp. |
| Offline Mode | Erfordert Service Worker + Sync Queue. Irrelevant für Demo-Umgebung. |
| Pricing / Feature Flags | Business Logic, nicht Produkt-Validierung. |

---

## 8 — Der Prototyp ist erfolgreich, wenn …

**01 — Scan → Outcome in <1 Sekunde.** Token eingeben, Ergebnis sehen. Die Latenz muss zeigen, dass Real-Time-Resolution machbar ist — auch wenn der „Scan" ein Textfeld ist.

**02 — Kontext am Point of Action.** Der Staff-User sieht bei jedem Scan: Wer ist diese Person? Welches Tier? Wann läuft ab? Wie viele Guest Passes? Das ist der Unterschied zu „grün oder rot".

**03 — Dashboard zeigt das Unsichtbare.** Der Koordinator öffnet das Dashboard und sieht zum ersten Mal: Wie viele Entries heute? Wie viele Guest Passes? Wie viele Overrides? Wie viele Flags? Die Antwort auf „Wie viel verlieren wir?" wird zum ersten Mal sichtbar.

**04 — Datenmodell überlebt den Prototyp.** Die Tabellen, Relationen und Enum-Werte werden ohne Breaking Changes in Production übernommen.

---

*VAM Prototype Spec — Cuseum × BID Equity · April 2026*

file.content:
-- CRM Outbound: delivery table, retry bookkeeping, and trigger.
-- Phase 03 — task 03-01.
--
-- Schema additions:
--   institutions.crm_provider       TEXT CHECK (...)
--   institutions.crm_webhook_url    TEXT
--   institutions.crm_sync_interval_minutes INT DEFAULT 60 CHECK (>= 15)
--   crm_outbound_failures           table (visit_id FK, payload, attempt_count,
--                                   last_error, next_retry_at, institution_id)
--   DB trigger on visits AFTER INSERT → fires crm-outbound Edge Function
--     via pg_net when outcome IN ('admit','override')
--
-- NOTE: pg_cron is NOT available in Supabase Local for this project.
-- A reprocess_crm_failures() plpgsql function is defined so pgTAP tests
-- and manual tooling can drive retry logic without relying on an actual cron
-- job.  The real scheduling hook lives in the cron-tick Edge Function (03-03).

-- =========================================================================
-- institutions columns
-- =========================================================================
ALTER TABLE institutions
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS crm_provider TEXT
    CHECK (crm_provider IN ('blackbaud', 'tessitura', 'neonone', 'salesforce')),
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS crm_webhook_url TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS crm_sync_interval_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 60
    CHECK (crm_sync_interval_minutes >= 15);

-- =========================================================================
-- crm_outbound_failures — dead-letter + retry queue for failed webhook calls
-- =========================================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_outbound_failures (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  visit_id        UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES visits(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  payload         JSONB NOT NULL,
  attempt_count   INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  last_error      TEXT,
  next_retry_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now() + interval '1 minute',
  institution_id  UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES institutions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS crm_outbound_failures_retry_idx
  ON crm_outbound_failures (next_retry_at)
  WHERE attempt_count < 8;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS crm_outbound_failures_institution_idx
  ON crm_outbound_failures (institution_id);

ALTER TABLE crm_outbound_failures ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Service-role edge functions bypass RLS; no policy needed for Edge Functions.
-- Add a policy so dashboard users can read their own institution's failures.
CREATE POLICY crm_outbound_failures_isolation ON crm_outbound_failures
  FOR ALL
  USING (institution_id = current_institution_id())
  WITH CHECK (institution_id = current_institution_id());

-- =========================================================================
-- Trigger function: fire crm-outbound Edge Function via pg_net
-- =========================================================================
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION trigger_crm_outbound()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_institution    RECORD;
  v_member         RECORD;
  v_payload        JSONB;
  v_functions_url  TEXT;
  v_service_key    TEXT;
BEGIN
  -- Only fire for admit / override outcomes that have a configured CRM URL.
  IF NEW.outcome NOT IN ('admit', 'override') THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  SELECT id, name, crm_webhook_url, crm_provider
  INTO v_institution
  FROM institutions

2	-- Phase 03 — task 03-03.
3	--
4	-- Schema additions:
5	--   institutions.gpr_conversion_window_days INT DEFAULT 30
6	--   members.sms_opt_out                     BOOLEAN DEFAULT false
7	--   visits.sms_triggered_at                 already present (init schema)
8	--
9	-- Functions:
10	--   crm_attribute_member(p_institution_id UUID, p_payload JSONB)
11	--     — pure SQL attribution logic, called by both pgTAP and the Edge Function.
12	--   cron_tick()
13	--     — processes eligible guest-pass admits for post-visit SMS.
14	--       Callable from pgTAP tests instead of depending on actual pg_cron timing.
15	--
16	-- NOTE: pg_cron is NOT enabled in Supabase Local.  The plpgsql cron_tick()
17	-- function provides the same scheduling semantics and is invoked by the
18	-- cron-tick Edge Function.
19	
20	-- =========================================================================
21	-- Schema additions
22	-- =========================================================================
23	
24	ALTER TABLE institutions
25	  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS gpr_conversion_window_days INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 30;
26	
27	ALTER TABLE members
28	  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS sms_opt_out BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false;
29	
30	-- =========================================================================
31	-- crm_attribute_member — pure attribution logic.
32	-- Called from the crm-webhook Edge Function on member.created events.
33	-- Also callable directly from pgTAP.
34	--
35	-- Strategy:
36	--   1. token_match: payload->>'referral_token' matches guests.guest_token.
37	--      Updates the FIRST open gpr_conversions row for that guest.
38	--   2. phone_match: payload->>'phone' matches guests.phone.
39	--      Only applies within the institution's gpr_conversion_window_days.
40	--
41	-- Returns JSONB describing what attribution (if any) was performed.
42	-- =========================================================================
43	
44	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION crm_attribute_member(
45	  p_institution_id UUID,
46	  p_payload        JSONB
47	)
48	RETURNS JSONB
49	LANGUAGE plpgsql
50	SECURITY DEFINER
51	AS $$
52	DECLARE
53	  v_referral_token  TEXT;
54	  v_phone           TEXT;
55	  v_guest_id        UUID;
56	  v_conv_id         UUID;
57	  v_crm_id          TEXT;
58	  v_window_days     INT;
59	BEGIN
60	  v_referral_token := p_payload->>'referral_token';
61	  v_phone          := p_payload->>'phone';
62	  v_crm_id         := COALESCE(p_payload->>'external_crm_id', p_payload->>'member_id', '');
63	
64	  -- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
65	  -- 1. Token match — highest priority.
66	  -- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
67	  IF v_referral_token IS NOT NULL AND v_referral_token <> '' THEN
68	    SELECT g.id INTO v_guest_id
69	    FROM guests g
70	    WHERE g.guest_token = v_referral_token
71	      AND g.institution_id = p_institution_id
72	    LIMIT 1;
73	
74	    IF v_guest_id IS NOT NULL THEN
75	      UPDATE gpr_conversions
76	      SET
77	        attribution_method = 'token_match',
78	        conversion_crm_id  = v_crm_id,
79	        converted_at       = now()
80	      WHERE guest_id   = v_guest_id

file.content:
-- Post-Visit SMS Scheduling + GPR+ Conversion Window.
-- Phase 03 — task 03-03.
--
-- Schema additions:
--   institutions.gpr_conversion_window_days INT DEFAULT 30
--   members.sms_opt_out                     BOOLEAN DEFAULT false
--   visits.sms_triggered_at                 already present (init schema)
--
-- Functions:
--   crm_attribute_member(p_institution_id UUID, p_payload JSONB)
--     — pure SQL attribution logic, called by both pgTAP and the Edge Function.
--   cron_tick()
--     — processes eligible guest-pass admits for post-visit SMS.
--       Callable from pgTAP tests instead of depending on actual pg_cron timing.
--
-- NOTE: pg_cron is NOT enabled in Supabase Local.  The plpgsql cron_tick()
-- function provides the same scheduling semantics and is invoked by the
-- cron-tick Edge Function.

-- =========================================================================
-- Schema additions
-- =========================================================================

ALTER TABLE institutions
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS gpr_conversion_window_days INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 30;

ALTER TABLE members
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS sms_opt_out BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false;

-- =========================================================================
-- crm_attribute_member — pure attribution logic.
-- Called from the crm-webhook Edge Function on member.created events.
-- Also callable directly from pgTAP.
--
-- Strategy:
--   1. token_match: payload->>'referral_token' matches guests.guest_token.
--      Updates the FIRST open gpr_conversions row for that guest.
--   2. phone_match: payload->>'phone' matches guests.phone.
--      Only applies within the institution's gpr_conversion_window_days.
--
-- Returns JSONB describing what attribution (if any) was performed.
-- =========================================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION crm_attribute_member(
  p_institution_id UUID,
  p_payload        JSONB
)
RETURNS JSONB
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_referral_token  TEXT;
  v_phone           TEXT;
  v_guest_id        UUID;
  v_conv_id         UUID;
  v_crm_id          TEXT;
  v_window_days     INT;
BEGIN
  v_referral_token := p_payload->>'referral_token';
  v_phone          := p_payload->>'phone';
  v_crm_id         := COALESCE(p_payload->>'external_crm_id', p_payload->>'member_id', '');

  -- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  -- 1. Token match — highest priority.
  -- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  IF v_referral_token IS NOT NULL AND v_referral_token <> '' THEN
    SELECT g.id INTO v_guest_id
    FROM guests g
    WHERE g.guest_token = v_referral_token
      AND g.institution_id = p_institution_id
    LIMIT 1;

    IF v_guest_id IS NOT NULL THEN
      UPDATE gpr_conversions
      SET
        attribution_method = 'token_match',
        conversion_crm_id  = v_crm_id,
        converted_at       = now()
      WHERE guest_id   = v_guest_id

input.content:
# Plan — Example SPEC for VAM integration-status dashboard feature

## Context

Next dogfood test for dirigent's **hybrid route**: re-enter the existing `cuseum-vam-full` repo (dirigent's rc3 output) with a feature-addition spec and see whether hybrid handles extending dirigent's own prior work. Per `project_hybrid_route_auth_followup.md` the originally-queued test was auth; user pivoted to a smaller, observable feature: **surface SMS and CRM inbound/outbound status in the operator dashboard**.

The SPEC also doubles as a vehicle for testing whether dirigent respects the cuseum-vam-full **bid-harness** (portfolio engineering standards). ## SPEC to write

**Full SPEC content (drafted below — will be written to that path after ExitPlanMode):**

---

```markdown
# VAM — Integration Status Panel Spec

*Add-on feature for the existing `cuseum-vam-full` dashboard. Surfaces the state of the SMS pipeline (outbound) and CRM pipelines (outbound webhook + inbound webhook) so the membership coordinator can see whether integrations are healthy — without opening the Supabase logs.*

**Status:** Draft · **Scope:** 3-5 days · **Route:** Hybrid (feature-addition to existing dirigent-built repo)

---

## 0 — Why this feature

Today the coordinator can see *visits* in the dashboard (entries, flags, guest passes). What they cannot see is whether the **outgoing integrations that visits trigger** actually succeeded:

- Did the CRM webhook POST for yesterday's admits go through?
- How many post-visit SMS sends are queued / sent / opted-out?
- Did any CRM inbound webhooks come in overnight? Attribution matches?

When a coordinator asks "did Blackbaud get today's admits?" the answer currently requires opening Supabase logs. This feature moves that answer into the dashboard itself, via a single **Integration Status** page.

## 1 — Scope

### In scope

- A new dashboard page at `/integrations` under the existing `(dashboard)` route group
- Three panels on that page: **CRM Outbound**, **SMS Outbound**, **CRM Inbound**
- Each panel shows: current-window counts (today), last event timestamp, failure count, last error message, a "recent events" table (last 20)
- A manual retry control on CRM Outbound panel (calls existing `reprocess_crm_failures()` via a new `/api/integrations/retry-crm` Next.js route handler)
- Read-only queries against existing tables — **no new domain tables**
- Realtime updates via the existing Supabase Realtime subscription pattern used by `RealtimeBadge.tsx`

### Out of scope (explicit)

- Adding retry logic for SMS (only CRM already has a retry table)
- Historical graphs / time-series beyond "today" and "last 7 days"
- Alerting / notifications (email, Slack)
- Multi-institution views — assume the existing `current_institution_id()` RLS already scopes data
- Authentication / login changes — dashboard auth is tracked separately in `project_hybrid_route_auth_followup.md`
- Any change to Edge Functions themselves — consumers only
- New SMS outbound tracking columns — use `visits.sms_triggered_at` that already exists

## 2 — Data sources (read-only against existing schema)

**CRM Outbound:**
- `crm_outbound_failures` (exists) — `visit_id`, `attempt_count`, `last_error`, `next_retry_at`, `created_at`, `institution_id`
- `visits` (exists) — count of `outcome IN ('admit','override')` since midnight as the denominator (what *should* have fired)

**SMS Outbound:**
- `visits.sms_triggered_at` (exists) — count non-null as "sent"
- `visits` where `sms_triggered_at IS NULL AND outcome = 'admit' AND credential_type = 'guest_pass'` as "pending" (matches the `cron_tick()` eligibility rule)
- `members.sms_opt_out` (exists) — count of opted-out members as a fixed KPI

**CRM Inbound:**
- `gpr_conversions` table (exists, from `_seed_phase03_gpr_fixtures.sql`) — attribution results
- A new lightweight append-only log table is required since `crm-webhook` today does not persist receipt metadata. **Only this one new table is allowed in scope** (see Data Changes below).

## 3 — Data changes (minimal)

### New table — `crm_inbound_events`

Rationale: the `crm-webhook` Edge Function currently drops received payloads after attribution. To show "last inbound event" + verification status in the dashboard, we need a persistence point.

```sql
CREATE TABLE crm_inbound_events (
  id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  institution_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES institutions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  provider         TEXT NOT NULL,        -- echoes institutions.crm_provider
  event_type       TEXT NOT NULL,        -- 'member.created', 'member.updated', etc.
  received_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  signature_valid  BOOLEAN NOT NULL,     -- HMAC verification result
  attribution_result TEXT,               -- 'token_match' | 'phone_match' | 'none' | NULL on error
  error_message    TEXT,                 -- NULL on success
  payload_summary  JSONB                 -- truncated; NOT the full payload
);

CREATE INDEX crm_inbound_events_institution_time_idx
  ON crm_inbound_events (institution_id, received_at DESC);

ALTER TABLE crm_inbound_events ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY crm_inbound_events_isolation ON crm_inbound_events
  FOR ALL
  USING (institution_id = current_institution_id())
  WITH CHECK (institution_id = current_institution_id());
```

### Edge Function changes — `crm-webhook/index.ts`

Append an insert into `crm_inbound_events` at the end of each request (both success and failure paths). Do **not** change attribution logic. Do **not** store full payloads — store only `{type, external_id, phone_present: bool, token_present: bool}` as `payload_summary`.

### Migration file

New migration file: `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_add_crm_inbound_events.sql`. Timestamp convention matches existing files (`YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*.sql`).

## 4 — UI

### Route

`/integrations` under the existing `(dashboard)` group. Link from the existing `NavLinks.tsx`.

### Page layout

Three `KpiCard`-based sections (mirror the pattern already used on the main dashboard), each with:
- A header (CRM Outbound / SMS Outbound / CRM Inbound)
- 3-4 KPI numbers (mirror `KpiCard.tsx`)
- A "last event" line with relative timestamp
- A collapsible "Recent events" table (mirror `EntryLogTable.tsx` styling)
- For CRM Outbound: a "Retry all failed" button wired to `/api/integrations/retry-crm`

### KPIs per panel

| Panel | KPIs |
|---|---|
| CRM Outbound | Sent today · Failures today · Retrying · Dead-lettered (attempt_count ≥ 8) |
| SMS Outbound | Sent today · Pending · Opted out · (last cron_tick run time) |
| CRM Inbound | Received today · Signature failures today · Attributed today · Not attributed today |

### Recent events table columns

- CRM Outbound: `visit_id` (short), `attempt_count`, `last_error` (truncated), `next_retry_at`, `created_at`
- SMS Outbound: `visit_id` (short), `credential_type`, `scanned_at`, `sms_triggered_at`, status chip
- CRM Inbound: `event_type`, `provider`, `received_at`, `signature_valid` badge, `attribution_result` chip, `error_message` (truncated)

### Realtime

Subscribe to `crm_outbound_failures` inserts, `visits` updates where `sms_triggered_at` changed, and `crm_inbound_events` inserts. Reuse the existing Supabase Realtime client initialization pattern (see `RealtimeBadge.tsx`).

## 5 — Success criteria

1. Navigating to `/integrations` shows three panels populated from live data within 1 second on the demo dataset.
2. After triggering an admit in the scan app, the CRM Outbound "Sent today" counter increments within 2 seconds (realtime subscription works).
3. If the CRM webhook URL is set to an invalid host, the resulting row appears in CRM Outbound recent events within 2 seconds with the HTTP error as `last_error`.
4. Clicking "Retry all failed" triggers `reprocess_crm_failures()` and the counter decrements as failures succeed.
5. Sending a mock inbound webhook POST to `/crm-webhook` produces a new row in CRM Inbound recent events with `signature_valid` reflecting the HMAC check result.
6. With `members.sms_opt_out = true` for a member, a new admit for them does NOT increment SMS "Pending" (the cron_tick eligibility rule is already correct — we just need to display it).

## 6 — Constraints — engineering standards

This repo is governed by the portfolio engineering standards in **`harness-docs/engineering-standards.md`**. All code produced for this feature MUST comply with **[ENFORCED]** rules, specifically:

- Conventional Commits for every commit (`feat(dashboard): …`, `feat(db): …`, etc.)
- Tests alongside new code — pgTAP for the migration + trigger changes, vitest for the Next.js route handler, component test for the page
- No `any` in TypeScript; `strict: true` must stay on
- Structured logging for any new server code (pino in the route handler, JSON logs in Edge Function changes)
- No secrets in code (HMAC secret stays in `Deno.env`)
- Run the `standards-reviewer` agent on the staged diff before each `git commit`
- Small, focused PRs — the feature may land as 3-4 atomic commits (migration · webhook persistence · API route · UI) not one mega-commit

The **[RECOMMENDED]** rules should be followed when practical; flag deviations in PR descriptions.

## 7 — Testing

- **pgTAP** (existing harness): one test file for the `crm_inbound_events` table (insert, RLS, index presence).
- **Deno test** (existing harness): add tests to `supabase/functions/tests/` covering the webhook's new persistence step, success + signature-failure paths.
- **vitest** (existing harness in `apps/dashboard/`): test the `/api/integrations/retry-crm` route handler (success, no-failures-to-retry, unauthorized).
- **Component test**: render `/integrations` against a mocked Supabase client with fixture rows in each of the three data sources; assert KPI values and recent-event row counts.
- **Manual smoke**: follow success criteria 1-6 against a running `supabase start` stack plus `pnpm dev` on the dashboard.

## 8 — Verification before ship

- `supabase db reset && supabase test db` — pgTAP passes
- `deno test supabase/functions/` — Edge Function tests pass
- `pnpm --filter dashboard test` — vitest passes
- `pnpm --filter dashboard lint && pnpm --filter dashboard typecheck` — standards linter + strict TS pass
- `standards-reviewer` agent returns no [MUST] violations on staged diff
- Manual smoke test against demo seed dataset
```

---

## Critical files dirigent will touch (for reviewer context)

- `supabase/migrations/<new>_add_crm_inbound_events.sql` (new)
- `supabase/functions/crm-webhook/index.ts` (extend)
- `supabase/functions/tests/crm-webhook.test.ts` (extend)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/integrations/page.tsx` (new)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/integrations/*-panel.tsx` (new, 3 files)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/api/integrations/retry-crm/route.ts` (new)
- `apps/dashboard/src/components/NavLinks.tsx` (extend — add /integrations link)

## Why this is a good hybrid-route test (beyond the auth followup)

- **Bounded scope touching multiple layers** (DB + Edge Function + dashboard) — ideal hybrid territory
- **Exercises quick-scan's job**: scan must find the 3 existing integration points (crm_outbound_failures, visits.sms_triggered_at, crm-webhook) without dragging in unrelated GPR/fraud code
- **Standards harness pickup test**: will the standards-reviewer agent run before commit? Will the structured-logging / conventional-commits / tests-with-new-code rules survive dirigent's plan-execute pipeline?
- **One deliberate new table** tests whether atomic-commit discipline holds when migration + code changes must land together
- **Smaller than the auth dogfood** → faster iteration, cheaper run (target <$20 vs rc3's $87)

## Verification (once the SPEC file exists)

- `ls ~/projects/outbid-dirigent/example-specs/cuseum-vam-integration-status.md` — file exists
- Word count ~700 lines / 8 sections — scannable but complete
- User-editable — user will likely tighten scope further before running the dogfood

## Out of scope for this planning turn

- Actually running the dogfood — that's the next session
- Creating a Claude Code slash command to auto-launch dirigent with this SPEC
- Updating the memory file — will do that after the dogfood runs, recording actual observations

# Plan — Example SPEC for VAM integration-status dashboard feature

## Context

Next dogfood test for dirigent's **hybrid route**: re-enter the existing `cuseum-vam-full` repo (dirigent's rc3 output) with a feature-addition spec and see whether hybrid handles extending dirigent's own prior work. Per `project_hybrid_route_auth_followup.md` the originally-queued test was auth; user pivoted to a smaller, observable feature: **surface SMS and CRM inbound/outbound status in the operator dashboard**.

The SPEC also doubles as a vehicle for testing whether dirigent respects the cuseum-vam-full **bid-harness** (portfolio engineering standards). Approach: option 1+2 mix — cite `harness-docs/engineering-standards.md` from inside the SPEC (planner reads SPEC directly) and rely on CLAUDE.md inheritance for subagent pickup (bid-harness already injects the standards block between `<!-- BEGIN:bid-harness -->` markers).

## Existing code this feature builds on

From the rc3 VAM build (read during plan):

- `supabase/migrations/20260418140600_add_crm_outbound.sql` — trigger-fired `crm-outbound` Edge Function, `crm_outbound_failures` table with retry bookkeeping (attempt_count, last_error, next_retry_at)
- `supabase/migrations/20260418140700_add_post_visit_sms_trigger.sql` — `cron_tick()` SQL fn, `members.sms_opt_out`, `visits.sms_triggered_at`
- `supabase/functions/crm-outbound/index.ts` — CRM POST with retry/dead-letter
- `supabase/functions/crm-webhook/index.ts` — inbound CRM webhook (member.created attribution)
- `supabase/functions/renewal-sms/index.ts` — Twilio post-visit SMS send
- `supabase/functions/cron-tick/index.ts` — pg_cron stand-in, drives SMS scheduling
- `apps/dashboard/src/components/` — `KpiCard.tsx`, `EntryLogTable.tsx`, `FlagRow.tsx`, `RealtimeBadge.tsx` (pattern reference for new panel)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/` — existing route group

**Gap:** none of this is surfaced in the dashboard. Operators cannot see whether CRM webhook calls succeeded, how many SMS sends are pending, which integrations failed, or what the last CRM push/pull was.

## SPEC to write

**Target path:** `/Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent/example-specs/cuseum-vam-integration-status.md`

**Full SPEC content (drafted below — will be written to that path after ExitPlanMode):**

---

```markdown
# VAM — Integration Status Panel Spec

*Add-on feature for the existing `cuseum-vam-full` dashboard. Surfaces the state of the SMS pipeline (outbound) and CRM pipelines (outbound webhook + inbound webhook) so the membership coordinator can see whether integrations are healthy — without opening the Supabase logs.*

**Status:** Draft · **Scope:** 3-5 days · **Route:** Hybrid (feature-addition to existing dirigent-built repo)

---

## 0 — Why this feature

Today the coordinator can see *visits* in the dashboard (entries, flags, guest passes). What they cannot see is whether the **outgoing integrations that visits trigger** actually succeeded:

- Did the CRM webhook POST for yesterday's admits go through?
- How many post-visit SMS sends are queued / sent / opted-out?
- Did any CRM inbound webhooks come in overnight? Attribution matches?

When a coordinator asks "did Blackbaud get today's admits?" the answer currently requires opening Supabase logs. This feature moves that answer into the dashboard itself, via a single **Integration Status** page.

## 1 — Scope

### In scope

- A new dashboard page at `/integrations` under the existing `(dashboard)` route group
- Three panels on that page: **CRM Outbound**, **SMS Outbound**, **CRM Inbound**
- Each panel shows: current-window counts (today), last event timestamp, failure count, last error message, a "recent events" table (last 20)
- A manual retry control on CRM Outbound panel (calls existing `reprocess_crm_failures()` via a new `/api/integrations/retry-crm` Next.js route handler)
- Read-only queries against existing tables — **no new domain tables**
- Realtime updates via the existing Supabase Realtime subscription pattern used by `RealtimeBadge.tsx`

### Out of scope (explicit)

- Adding retry logic for SMS (only CRM already has a retry table)
- Historical graphs / time-series beyond "today" and "last 7 days"
- Alerting / notifications (email, Slack)
- Multi-institution views — assume the existing `current_institution_id()` RLS already scopes data
- Authentication / login changes — dashboard auth is tracked separately in `project_hybrid_route_auth_followup.md`
- Any change to Edge Functions themselves — consumers only
- New SMS outbound tracking columns — use `visits.sms_triggered_at` that already exists

## 2 — Data sources (read-only against existing schema)

**CRM Outbound:**
- `crm_outbound_failures` (exists) — `visit_id`, `attempt_count`, `last_error`, `next_retry_at`, `created_at`, `institution_id`
- `visits` (exists) — count of `outcome IN ('admit','override')` since midnight as the denominator (what *should* have fired)

**SMS Outbound:**
- `visits.sms_triggered_at` (exists) — count non-null as "sent"
- `visits` where `sms_triggered_at IS NULL AND outcome = 'admit' AND credential_type = 'guest_pass'` as "pending" (matches the `cron_tick()` eligibility rule)
- `members.sms_opt_out` (exists) — count of opted-out members as a fixed KPI

**CRM Inbound:**
- `gpr_conversions` table (exists, from `_seed_phase03_gpr_fixtures.sql`) — attribution results
- A new lightweight append-only log table is required since `crm-webhook` today does not persist receipt metadata. **Only this one new table is allowed in scope** (see Data Changes below).

## 3 — Data changes (minimal)

### New table — `crm_inbound_events`

Rationale: the `crm-webhook` Edge Function currently drops received payloads after attribution. To show "last inbound event" + verification status in the dashboard, we need a persistence point.

```sql
CREATE TABLE crm_inbound_events (
  id               UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  institution_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES institutions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  provider         TEXT NOT NULL,        -- echoes institutions.crm_provider
  event_type       TEXT NOT NULL,        -- 'member.created', 'member.updated', etc.
  received_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  signature_valid  BOOLEAN NOT NULL,     -- HMAC verification result
  attribution_result TEXT,               -- 'token_match' | 'phone_match' | 'none' | NULL on error
  error_message    TEXT,                 -- NULL on success
  payload_summary  JSONB                 -- truncated; NOT the full payload
);

CREATE INDEX crm_inbound_events_institution_time_idx
  ON crm_inbound_events (institution_id, received_at DESC);

ALTER TABLE crm_inbound_events ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY crm_inbound_events_isolation ON crm_inbound_events
  FOR ALL
  USING (institution_id = current_institution_id())
  WITH CHECK (institution_id = current_institution_id());
```

### Edge Function changes — `crm-webhook/index.ts`

Append an insert into `crm_inbound_events` at the end of each request (both success and failure paths). Do **not** change attribution logic. Do **not** store full payloads — store only `{type, external_id, phone_present: bool, token_present: bool}` as `payload_summary`.

### Migration file

New migration file: `supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_add_crm_inbound_events.sql`. Timestamp convention matches existing files (`YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*.sql`).

## 4 — UI

### Route

`/integrations` under the existing `(dashboard)` group. Link from the existing `NavLinks.tsx`.

### Page layout

Three `KpiCard`-based sections (mirror the pattern already used on the main dashboard), each with:
- A header (CRM Outbound / SMS Outbound / CRM Inbound)
- 3-4 KPI numbers (mirror `KpiCard.tsx`)
- A "last event" line with relative timestamp
- A collapsible "Recent events" table (mirror `EntryLogTable.tsx` styling)
- For CRM Outbound: a "Retry all failed" button wired to `/api/integrations/retry-crm`

### KPIs per panel

| Panel | KPIs |
|---|---|
| CRM Outbound | Sent today · Failures today · Retrying · Dead-lettered (attempt_count ≥ 8) |
| SMS Outbound | Sent today · Pending · Opted out · (last cron_tick run time) |
| CRM Inbound | Received today · Signature failures today · Attributed today · Not attributed today |

### Recent events table columns

- CRM Outbound: `visit_id` (short), `attempt_count`, `last_error` (truncated), `next_retry_at`, `created_at`
- SMS Outbound: `visit_id` (short), `credential_type`, `scanned_at`, `sms_triggered_at`, status chip
- CRM Inbound: `event_type`, `provider`, `received_at`, `signature_valid` badge, `attribution_result` chip, `error_message` (truncated)

### Realtime

Subscribe to `crm_outbound_failures` inserts, `visits` updates where `sms_triggered_at` changed, and `crm_inbound_events` inserts. Reuse the existing Supabase Realtime client initialization pattern (see `RealtimeBadge.tsx`).

## 5 — Success criteria

1. Navigating to `/integrations` shows three panels populated from live data within 1 second on the demo dataset.
2. After triggering an admit in the scan app, the CRM Outbound "Sent today" counter increments within 2 seconds (realtime subscription works).
3. If the CRM webhook URL is set to an invalid host, the resulting row appears in CRM Outbound recent events within 2 seconds with the HTTP error as `last_error`.
4. Clicking "Retry all failed" triggers `reprocess_crm_failures()` and the counter decrements as failures succeed.
5. Sending a mock inbound webhook POST to `/crm-webhook` produces a new row in CRM Inbound recent events with `signature_valid` reflecting the HMAC check result.
6. With `members.sms_opt_out = true` for a member, a new admit for them does NOT increment SMS "Pending" (the cron_tick eligibility rule is already correct — we just need to display it).

## 6 — Constraints — engineering standards

This repo is governed by the portfolio engineering standards in **`harness-docs/engineering-standards.md`**. All code produced for this feature MUST comply with **[ENFORCED]** rules, specifically:

- Conventional Commits for every commit (`feat(dashboard): …`, `feat(db): …`, etc.)
- Tests alongside new code — pgTAP for the migration + trigger changes, vitest for the Next.js route handler, component test for the page
- No `any` in TypeScript; `strict: true` must stay on
- Structured logging for any new server code (pino in the route handler, JSON logs in Edge Function changes)
- No secrets in code (HMAC secret stays in `Deno.env`)
- Run the `standards-reviewer` agent on the staged diff before each `git commit`
- Small, focused PRs — the feature may land as 3-4 atomic commits (migration · webhook persistence · API route · UI) not one mega-commit

The **[RECOMMENDED]** rules should be followed when practical; flag deviations in PR descriptions.

## 7 — Testing

- **pgTAP** (existing harness): one test file for the `crm_inbound_events` table (insert, RLS, index presence).
- **Deno test** (existing harness): add tests to `supabase/functions/tests/` covering the webhook's new persistence step, success + signature-failure paths.
- **vitest** (existing harness in `apps/dashboard/`): test the `/api/integrations/retry-crm` route handler (success, no-failures-to-retry, unauthorized).
- **Component test**: render `/integrations` against a mocked Supabase client with fixture rows in each of the three data sources; assert KPI values and recent-event row counts.
- **Manual smoke**: follow success criteria 1-6 against a running `supabase start` stack plus `pnpm dev` on the dashboard.

## 8 — Verification before ship

- `supabase db reset && supabase test db` — pgTAP passes
- `deno test supabase/functions/` — Edge Function tests pass
- `pnpm --filter dashboard test` — vitest passes
- `pnpm --filter dashboard lint && pnpm --filter dashboard typecheck` — standards linter + strict TS pass
- `standards-reviewer` agent returns no [MUST] violations on staged diff
- Manual smoke test against demo seed dataset
```

---

## Critical files dirigent will touch (for reviewer context)

- `supabase/migrations/<new>_add_crm_inbound_events.sql` (new)
- `supabase/functions/crm-webhook/index.ts` (extend)
- `supabase/functions/tests/crm-webhook.test.ts` (extend)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/integrations/page.tsx` (new)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/integrations/*-panel.tsx` (new, 3 files)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/api/integrations/retry-crm/route.ts` (new)
- `apps/dashboard/src/components/NavLinks.tsx` (extend — add /integrations link)

## Why this is a good hybrid-route test (beyond the auth followup)

- **Bounded scope touching multiple layers** (DB + Edge Function + dashboard) — ideal hybrid territory
- **Exercises quick-scan's job**: scan must find the 3 existing integration points (crm_outbound_failures, visits.sms_triggered_at, crm-webhook) without dragging in unrelated GPR/fraud code
- **Standards harness pickup test**: will the standards-reviewer agent run before commit? Will the structured-logging / conventional-commits / tests-with-new-code rules survive dirigent's plan-execute pipeline?
- **One deliberate new table** tests whether atomic-commit discipline holds when migration + code changes must land together
- **Smaller than the auth dogfood** → faster iteration, cheaper run (target <$20 vs rc3's $87)

## Verification (once the SPEC file exists)

- `ls ~/projects/outbid-dirigent/example-specs/cuseum-vam-integration-status.md` — file exists
- Word count ~700 lines / 8 sections — scannable but complete
- User-editable — user will likely tighten scope further before running the dogfood

## Out of scope for this planning turn

- Actually running the dogfood — that's the next session
- Creating a Claude Code slash command to auto-launch dirigent with this SPEC
- Updating the memory file — will do that after the dogfood runs, recording actual observations

# Plan — Two example SPECs for VAM integration-status dashboard feature

## Context

Next dogfood for dirigent tests feature-addition on an existing dirigent-built codebase (cuseum-vam-full, rc3 output). User wants **two** example SPECs written to `example-specs/` so the dogfood can test both quality tiers against dirigent:

1. **Polished SPEC** — what a product-minded engineer writes after reading spec-authoring best practices
2. **Realistic hand-written SPEC** — what an actual coordinator or first-time user scribbles down: stream of consciousness, missing structure, vague on implementation, possibly over- or under-scoped

This pair lets the dogfood measure whether hybrid-route handling degrades gracefully on messy input, or whether dirigent silently drops requirements when the spec is informal. Neither SPEC mentions routes — routing is dirigent's internal concern; the SPEC is just what the user wants built.

The SPECs also test **option 1+2 harness integration**: the polished SPEC cites `harness-docs/engineering-standards.md` directly; the realistic one doesn't know the harness exists, so we rely purely on CLAUDE.md inheritance (bid-harness block) for subagent pickup.

## Feature both SPECs describe

Surface SMS and CRM integration health in the operator dashboard. The VAM rc3 build has `crm-outbound` (trigger → pg_net → Edge Function → `crm_outbound_failures` table), `crm-webhook` (inbound member.created), and `renewal-sms` + `cron_tick()` (post-visit SMS). None of it is visible in the dashboard today — operators open Supabase logs to answer "did Blackbaud get today's admits?". This feature closes that loop.

## Existing code both SPECs implicitly or explicitly reference

- `supabase/migrations/20260418140600_add_crm_outbound.sql` — `crm_outbound_failures` with `attempt_count`, `last_error`, `next_retry_at`; `reprocess_crm_failures()`
- `supabase/migrations/20260418140700_add_post_visit_sms_trigger.sql` — `cron_tick()`, `members.sms_opt_out`, `visits.sms_triggered_at`
- `supabase/functions/{crm-outbound, crm-webhook, renewal-sms, cron-tick}/index.ts`
- `apps/dashboard/src/components/` — `KpiCard`, `EntryLogTable`, `FlagRow`, `RealtimeBadge`, `NavLinks` (pattern references)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/` — existing route group
- `harness-docs/engineering-standards.md` — portfolio standards; injected into CLAUDE.md via bid-harness plugin

## Files to write

Two files under `/Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent/example-specs/`:

1. `cuseum-vam-integration-status.md` — polished
2. `cuseum-vam-integration-status-raw.md` — realistic hand-written

---

## SPEC 1 — Polished (cuseum-vam-integration-status.md)

```markdown
# VAM — Integration Status Panel

*Add-on feature for the existing `cuseum-vam-full` dashboard. Surfaces the state of the SMS pipeline and CRM pipelines (outbound + inbound webhooks) so the membership coordinator can see integration health without opening Supabase logs.*

**Status:** Draft · **Scope:** 3-5 days · **Demo-User:** 1 Institution

---

## 0 — Why this feature

Today the coordinator sees *visits* in the dashboard (entries, flags, guest passes). What they cannot see is whether the **outgoing integrations visits trigger** actually succeeded:

- Did the CRM webhook POST for today's admits go through?
- How many post-visit SMS sends are queued / sent / opted-out?
- Did any CRM inbound webhooks come in? Attribution matches?

When a coordinator asks "did Blackbaud get today's admits?" the answer currently requires Supabase logs. This feature moves that answer into the dashboard, via an **Integration Status** page.

## 1 — Scope

### In scope

- New dashboard page at `/integrations` under the existing `(dashboard)` route group
- Three panels: **CRM Outbound**, **SMS Outbound**, **CRM Inbound**
- Each panel: current-window KPIs (today), last event timestamp, failure count, last error, recent events table (last 20)
- Manual retry control on CRM Outbound (calls existing `reprocess_crm_failures()` via a new Next.js API route handler)
- Read-only queries against existing tables where possible — **one new persistence table** for inbound webhook receipts (today's webhook function drops payloads after attribution)
- Realtime updates via the existing Supabase Realtime pattern used by `RealtimeBadge.tsx`

### Out of scope (explicit)

- Retry logic for SMS (no retry table exists today; do not add one)
- Historical time-series beyond "today" and "last 7 days"
- Alerting / notifications (email, Slack, push)
- Multi-institution views — rely on existing `current_institution_id()` RLS
- Authentication / login changes — tracked separately
- Any change to Edge Function business logic — read-side only, plus one audit-trail insert in `crm-webhook`
- New SMS tracking columns — `visits.sms_triggered_at` already exists and is sufficient

## 2 — Data sources

**CRM Outbound:**
- `crm_outbound_failures` (exists) — `visit_id`, `attempt_count`, `last_error`, `next_retry_at`, `created_at`, `institution_id`
- `visits` (exists) — count of `outcome IN ('admit','override')` since midnight as denominator

**SMS Outbound:**
- `visits.sms_triggered_at` non-null = "sent"
- `visits WHERE sms_triggered_at IS NULL AND outcome = 'admit' AND credential_type = 'guest_pass'` = "pending" (matches existing `cron_tick()` eligibility)
- `members.sms_opt_out` — count of opted-out members

**CRM Inbound:**
- `gpr_conversions` (exists) — attribution results for the "Attributed today" KPI
- `crm_inbound_events` — new table (see Data Changes)

## 3 — Data changes (minimal)

### New table — `crm_inbound_events`

Rationale: `crm-webhook` today drops received payloads after attribution. To show "last inbound event" + verification status, we need a persistence point.

```sql
CREATE TABLE crm_inbound_events (
  id                 UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  institution_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES institutions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  provider           TEXT NOT NULL,
  event_type         TEXT NOT NULL,
  received_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  signature_valid    BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  attribution_result TEXT,
  error_message      TEXT,
  payload_summary    JSONB
);

CREATE INDEX crm_inbound_events_institution_time_idx
  ON crm_inbound_events (institution_id, received_at DESC);

ALTER TABLE crm_inbound_events ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY crm_inbound_events_isolation ON crm_inbound_events
  FOR ALL
  USING (institution_id = current_institution_id())
  WITH CHECK (institution_id = current_institution_id());
```

### Edge Function change — `crm-webhook/index.ts`

Append an insert into `crm_inbound_events` at the end of each request (success AND failure paths). Do NOT change attribution logic. Store only a small summary in `payload_summary` (e.g. `{type, external_id, phone_present: bool, token_present: bool}`) — never full payloads.

### Migration file

New file under `supabase/migrations/` matching existing `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*.sql` naming.

## 4 — UI

### Route

`/integrations` under `(dashboard)`. Link from `NavLinks.tsx`.

### Page layout

Three panels, each mirroring the `KpiCard` pattern already used on the main dashboard:

- Header (CRM Outbound / SMS Outbound / CRM Inbound)
- 3-4 KPI numbers
- "Last event" line with relative timestamp
- Collapsible "Recent events" table styled like `EntryLogTable.tsx`
- CRM Outbound panel: "Retry all failed" button → POST `/api/integrations/retry-crm`

### KPIs per panel

| Panel | KPIs |
|---|---|
| CRM Outbound | Sent today · Failures today · Retrying · Dead-lettered (attempt_count ≥ 8) |
| SMS Outbound | Sent today · Pending · Opted out · Last cron_tick run |
| CRM Inbound | Received today · Signature failures today · Attributed today · Not attributed today |

### Recent events columns

- CRM Outbound: `visit_id` (short) · `attempt_count` · `last_error` (truncated) · `next_retry_at` · `created_at`
- SMS Outbound: `visit_id` (short) · `credential_type` · `scanned_at` · `sms_triggered_at` · status chip
- CRM Inbound: `event_type` · `provider` · `received_at` · `signature_valid` badge · `attribution_result` chip · `error_message` (truncated)

### Realtime

Subscribe to inserts on `crm_outbound_failures`, updates on `visits` where `sms_triggered_at` changed, and inserts on `crm_inbound_events`. Reuse the Supabase Realtime init pattern from `RealtimeBadge.tsx`.

## 5 — Success criteria

1. `/integrations` renders three panels from live data within 1 second on the demo dataset.
2. After an admit in the scan app, CRM Outbound "Sent today" increments within 2 seconds (realtime works).
3. If `institutions.crm_webhook_url` is invalid, the resulting failure row appears in CRM Outbound recent events within 2 seconds with HTTP error as `last_error`.
4. Clicking "Retry all failed" triggers `reprocess_crm_failures()` and failures decrement as retries succeed.
5. A mock inbound POST to `/crm-webhook` produces a new `crm_inbound_events` row with `signature_valid` reflecting HMAC verification.
6. A member with `sms_opt_out = true` generates no increment in SMS "Pending" after an admit.

## 6 — Engineering standards

This repo is governed by portfolio engineering standards in `harness-docs/engineering-standards.md`. All code produced for this feature MUST comply with [ENFORCED] rules:

- Conventional Commits (`feat(dashboard): …`, `feat(db): …`)
- Tests alongside new code — pgTAP for migration + trigger, Deno test for Edge Function change, vitest for API route, component test for page
- TypeScript `strict: true`; no `any`
- Structured logging (pino in the API route; JSON logs in Edge Function)
- No secrets in code — HMAC secret stays in `Deno.env`
- `standards-reviewer` agent runs on staged diff before each commit
- Small focused PRs — land as 3-4 atomic commits (migration · webhook persistence · API route · UI), not a mega-commit

Recommended rules — follow when practical; note deviations in PR description.

## 7 — Testing

- **pgTAP**: `crm_inbound_events` table (insert, RLS isolation, index presence)
- **Deno test** (`supabase/functions/tests/`): webhook persistence on success AND signature-failure
- **vitest** (`apps/dashboard`): `/api/integrations/retry-crm` — success, no-failures, unauthorized
- **Component test**: render `/integrations` with mocked Supabase client + fixtures; assert KPI values and recent-event row counts
- **Manual smoke**: follow success criteria 1-6 against `supabase start` + `pnpm dev`

## 8 — Verification before ship

- `supabase db reset && supabase test db` — pgTAP passes
- `deno test supabase/functions/` — Edge Function tests pass
- `pnpm --filter dashboard test` — vitest passes
- `pnpm --filter dashboard lint && pnpm --filter dashboard typecheck` — pass
- `standards-reviewer` agent — no [MUST] violations on staged diff
- Manual smoke against demo seed dataset

---

*VAM Integration Status Spec — Cuseum × BID Equity · April 2026*
```

---

## SPEC 2 — Realistic hand-written (cuseum-vam-integration-status-raw.md)

```markdown
# integration status page for vam

ok so the dashboard shows visits and guest passes and flags which is great but the coordinators keep asking me "did blackbaud get today's visits" or "why didn't maria get her text message" and the only way to answer that right now is to ssh into supabase and look at logs which is obviously terrible.

we need a page in the dashboard where they can see if the integrations are working.

## what should be on it

- crm outbound — did the post-visit pushes to the crm actually go through today? if any failed, show them. there should be a button to retry failed ones.
- crm inbound — when the crm calls us back with new member data, did that work? did we attribute it correctly (token match, phone match, nothing)?
- sms — how many post-visit texts went out today, how many are pending, how many people opted out

for each of those three sections show the numbers for today + a list of recent events (like the last 20 or so). similar look to the main dashboard page, re-use the card thing and the table thing.

updates should be live, same way the main page updates when a new scan happens.

## the retry thing

for crm outbound failures, there's already a function in the db called `reprocess_crm_failures` or something like that. hook up a button that calls it.

## the inbound crm thing

right now when the crm posts to our webhook we process it but we don't save anything about the request itself, so we can't show "5 events received today" because we literally don't know. we need to add a small table that logs each inbound webhook call: what type of event, when it came in, whether the signature was valid, and whether attribution worked. don't store the full payload, just enough to show in the list. then have the webhook function insert a row every time.

## sms opt-outs

don't send texts to people who opted out obviously. but the opt-out logic already exists in cron_tick so we probably don't need to change anything, just surface the count.

## what i don't want

- no email alerts, no slack bots, no push notifications. just the dashboard page.
- don't rebuild the outbound retry system, it already exists.
- don't touch the login stuff, that's a separate conversation.
- don't add graphs showing the last 30 days or anything, just today.
- no multi-institution filter, the rls already does that.

## done when

coordinator opens the page and within a second sees three panels with actual numbers. triggers an admit in the scan app and the outbound counter ticks up. breaks the webhook url on purpose and sees the failure show up. clicks retry and watches the count go down.

also: tests for the new stuff (whatever the existing test setup uses — i think there's pgtap and vitest somewhere). and the new db migration has to follow the timestamp naming like the existing ones.

## files probably affected

- something under supabase/migrations/ (new)
- supabase/functions/crm-webhook/index.ts (add the insert)
- apps/dashboard — new page, new api route for the retry
- NavLinks.tsx or whatever — link to the new page

shouldn't be more than a few days of work.
```

---

## Why two versions

| Version | Tests |
|---|---|
| Polished | Does hybrid-route preserve structured requirements end-to-end? Does citing `engineering-standards.md` in the SPEC reach the coder? |
| Raw | Does dirigent degrade gracefully on informal specs? Does it invent scope, or does it ask / flag ambiguity? Does CLAUDE.md's bid-harness block alone carry the standards through without SPEC-side citation? |

Compare across the two runs: did the raw version drop requirements the polished one preserved? Did structured-logging show up in both runs, or only when the SPEC said so explicitly?

toolUseResult.content:
# Plan — Two example SPECs for VAM integration-status dashboard feature

## Context

Next dogfood for dirigent tests feature-addition on an existing dirigent-built codebase (cuseum-vam-full, rc3 output). User wants **two** example SPECs written to `example-specs/` so the dogfood can test both quality tiers against dirigent:

1. **Polished SPEC** — what a product-minded engineer writes after reading spec-authoring best practices
2. **Realistic hand-written SPEC** — what an actual coordinator or first-time user scribbles down: stream of consciousness, missing structure, vague on implementation, possibly over- or under-scoped

This pair lets the dogfood measure whether hybrid-route handling degrades gracefully on messy input, or whether dirigent silently drops requirements when the spec is informal. Neither SPEC mentions routes — routing is dirigent's internal concern; the SPEC is just what the user wants built.

The SPECs also test **option 1+2 harness integration**: the polished SPEC cites `harness-docs/engineering-standards.md` directly; the realistic one doesn't know the harness exists, so we rely purely on CLAUDE.md inheritance (bid-harness block) for subagent pickup.

## Feature both SPECs describe

Surface SMS and CRM integration health in the operator dashboard. The VAM rc3 build has `crm-outbound` (trigger → pg_net → Edge Function → `crm_outbound_failures` table), `crm-webhook` (inbound member.created), and `renewal-sms` + `cron_tick()` (post-visit SMS). None of it is visible in the dashboard today — operators open Supabase logs to answer "did Blackbaud get today's admits?". This feature closes that loop.

## Existing code both SPECs implicitly or explicitly reference

- `supabase/migrations/20260418140600_add_crm_outbound.sql` — `crm_outbound_failures` with `attempt_count`, `last_error`, `next_retry_at`; `reprocess_crm_failures()`
- `supabase/migrations/20260418140700_add_post_visit_sms_trigger.sql` — `cron_tick()`, `members.sms_opt_out`, `visits.sms_triggered_at`
- `supabase/functions/{crm-outbound, crm-webhook, renewal-sms, cron-tick}/index.ts`
- `apps/dashboard/src/components/` — `KpiCard`, `EntryLogTable`, `FlagRow`, `RealtimeBadge`, `NavLinks` (pattern references)
- `apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/` — existing route group
- `harness-docs/engineering-standards.md` — portfolio standards; injected into CLAUDE.md via bid-harness plugin

## Files to write

Two files under `/Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent/example-specs/`:

1. `cuseum-vam-integration-status.md` — polished
2. `cuseum-vam-integration-status-raw.md` — realistic hand-written

---

## SPEC 1 — Polished (cuseum-vam-integration-status.md)

```markdown
# VAM — Integration Status Panel

*Add-on feature for the existing `cuseum-vam-full` dashboard. Surfaces the state of the SMS pipeline and CRM pipelines (outbound + inbound webhooks) so the membership coordinator can see integration health without opening Supabase logs.*

**Status:** Draft · **Scope:** 3-5 days · **Demo-User:** 1 Institution

---

## 0 — Why this feature

Today the coordinator sees *visits* in the dashboard (entries, flags, guest passes). What they cannot see is whether the **outgoing integrations visits trigger** actually succeeded:

- Did the CRM webhook POST for today's admits go through?
- How many post-visit SMS sends are queued / sent / opted-out?
- Did any CRM inbound webhooks come in? Attribution matches?

When a coordinator asks "did Blackbaud get today's admits?" the answer currently requires Supabase logs. This feature moves that answer into the dashboard, via an **Integration Status** page.

## 1 — Scope

### In scope

- New dashboard page at `/integrations` under the existing `(dashboard)` route group
- Three panels: **CRM Outbound**, **SMS Outbound**, **CRM Inbound**
- Each panel: current-window KPIs (today), last event timestamp, failure count, last error, recent events table (last 20)
- Manual retry control on CRM Outbound (calls existing `reprocess_crm_failures()` via a new Next.js API route handler)
- Read-only queries against existing tables where possible — **one new persistence table** for inbound webhook receipts (today's webhook function drops payloads after attribution)
- Realtime updates via the existing Supabase Realtime pattern used by `RealtimeBadge.tsx`

### Out of scope (explicit)

- Retry logic for SMS (no retry table exists today; do not add one)
- Historical time-series beyond "today" and "last 7 days"
- Alerting / notifications (email, Slack, push)
- Multi-institution views — rely on existing `current_institution_id()` RLS
- Authentication / login changes — tracked separately
- Any change to Edge Function business logic — read-side only, plus one audit-trail insert in `crm-webhook`
- New SMS tracking columns — `visits.sms_triggered_at` already exists and is sufficient

## 2 — Data sources

**CRM Outbound:**
- `crm_outbound_failures` (exists) — `visit_id`, `attempt_count`, `last_error`, `next_retry_at`, `created_at`, `institution_id`
- `visits` (exists) — count of `outcome IN ('admit','override')` since midnight as denominator

**SMS Outbound:**
- `visits.sms_triggered_at` non-null = "sent"
- `visits WHERE sms_triggered_at IS NULL AND outcome = 'admit' AND credential_type = 'guest_pass'` = "pending" (matches existing `cron_tick()` eligibility)
- `members.sms_opt_out` — count of opted-out members

**CRM Inbound:**
- `gpr_conversions` (exists) — attribution results for the "Attributed today" KPI
- `crm_inbound_events` — new table (see Data Changes)

## 3 — Data changes (minimal)

### New table — `crm_inbound_events`

Rationale: `crm-webhook` today drops received payloads after attribution. To show "last inbound event" + verification status, we need a persistence point.

```sql
CREATE TABLE crm_inbound_events (
  id                 UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  institution_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES institutions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  provider           TEXT NOT NULL,
  event_type         TEXT NOT NULL,
  received_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  signature_valid    BOOLEAN NOT NULL,
  attribution_result TEXT,
  error_message      TEXT,
  payload_summary    JSONB
);

CREATE INDEX crm_inbound_events_institution_time_idx
  ON crm_inbound_events (institution_id, received_at DESC);

ALTER TABLE crm_inbound_events ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY crm_inbound_events_isolation ON crm_inbound_events
  FOR ALL
  USING (institution_id = current_institution_id())
  WITH CHECK (institution_id = current_institution_id());
```

### Edge Function change — `crm-webhook/index.ts`

Append an insert into `crm_inbound_events` at the end of each request (success AND failure paths). Do NOT change attribution logic. Store only a small summary in `payload_summary` (e.g. `{type, external_id, phone_present: bool, token_present: bool}`) — never full payloads.

### Migration file

New file under `supabase/migrations/` matching existing `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*.sql` naming.

## 4 — UI

### Route

`/integrations` under `(dashboard)`. Link from `NavLinks.tsx`.

### Page layout

Three panels, each mirroring the `KpiCard` pattern already used on the main dashboard:

- Header (CRM Outbound / SMS Outbound / CRM Inbound)
- 3-4 KPI numbers
- "Last event" line with relative timestamp
- Collapsible "Recent events" table styled like `EntryLogTable.tsx`
- CRM Outbound panel: "Retry all failed" button → POST `/api/integrations/retry-crm`

### KPIs per panel

| Panel | KPIs |
|---|---|
| CRM Outbound | Sent today · Failures today · Retrying · Dead-lettered (attempt_count ≥ 8) |
| SMS Outbound | Sent today · Pending · Opted out · Last cron_tick run |
| CRM Inbound | Received today · Signature failures today · Attributed today · Not attributed today |

### Recent events columns

- CRM Outbound: `visit_id` (short) · `attempt_count` · `last_error` (truncated) · `next_retry_at` · `created_at`
- SMS Outbound: `visit_id` (short) · `credential_type` · `scanned_at` · `sms_triggered_at` · status chip
- CRM Inbound: `event_type` · `provider` · `received_at` · `signature_valid` badge · `attribution_result` chip · `error_message` (truncated)

### Realtime

Subscribe to inserts on `crm_outbound_failures`, updates on `visits` where `sms_triggered_at` changed, and inserts on `crm_inbound_events`. Reuse the Supabase Realtime init pattern from `RealtimeBadge.tsx`.

## 5 — Success criteria

1. `/integrations` renders three panels from live data within 1 second on the demo dataset.
2. After an admit in the scan app, CRM Outbound "Sent today" increments within 2 seconds (realtime works).
3. If `institutions.crm_webhook_url` is invalid, the resulting failure row appears in CRM Outbound recent events within 2 seconds with HTTP error as `last_error`.
4. Clicking "Retry all failed" triggers `reprocess_crm_failures()` and failures decrement as retries succeed.
5. A mock inbound POST to `/crm-webhook` produces a new `crm_inbound_events` row with `signature_valid` reflecting HMAC verification.
6. A member with `sms_opt_out = true` generates no increment in SMS "Pending" after an admit.

## 6 — Engineering standards

This repo is governed by portfolio engineering standards in `harness-docs/engineering-standards.md`. All code produced for this feature MUST comply with [ENFORCED] rules:

- Conventional Commits (`feat(dashboard): …`, `feat(db): …`)
- Tests alongside new code — pgTAP for migration + trigger, Deno test for Edge Function change, vitest for API route, component test for page
- TypeScript `strict: true`; no `any`
- Structured logging (pino in the API route; JSON logs in Edge Function)
- No secrets in code — HMAC secret stays in `Deno.env`
- `standards-reviewer` agent runs on staged diff before each commit
- Small focused PRs — land as 3-4 atomic commits (migration · webhook persistence · API route · UI), not a mega-commit

Recommended rules — follow when practical; note deviations in PR description.

## 7 — Testing

- **pgTAP**: `crm_inbound_events` table (insert, RLS isolation, index presence)
- **Deno test** (`supabase/functions/tests/`): webhook persistence on success AND signature-failure
- **vitest** (`apps/dashboard`): `/api/integrations/retry-crm` — success, no-failures, unauthorized
- **Component test**: render `/integrations` with mocked Supabase client + fixtures; assert KPI values and recent-event row counts
- **Manual smoke**: follow success criteria 1-6 against `supabase start` + `pnpm dev`

## 8 — Verification before ship

- `supabase db reset && supabase test db` — pgTAP passes
- `deno test supabase/functions/` — Edge Function tests pass
- `pnpm --filter dashboard test` — vitest passes
- `pnpm --filter dashboard lint && pnpm --filter dashboard typecheck` — pass
- `standards-reviewer` agent — no [MUST] violations on staged diff
- Manual smoke against demo seed dataset

---

*VAM Integration Status Spec — Cuseum × BID Equity · April 2026*
```

---
